title: Warm Gooey Slovakian Sherry Pudding
description: A rich, comforting dessert featuring sweet sherry, savory mustard, and a hint of umami from shio-koji cure, served warm in individual mason jars. This Slovakian-inspired dessert is perfect for a cozy night in or a special occasion.

ingredients.name: Mustard Seeds
ingredients.notes: ground, such as brown or Dijon

instructions: In a medium saucepan, whisk together sherry, sugar, and 1/4 cup (60 ml) water until the sugar dissolves.
Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk together flour, shio-koji cure, and ground mustard seeds.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, butter, and a pinch of salt (if using).
Temper the egg mixture by slowly pouring the warm sherry mixture into the eggs, whisking continuously.
Add the flour mixture to the egg mixture and whisk until smooth.
Pour the mixture into 4-6 mason jars, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) of space at the top.
Place the mason jars in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the sides of the jars.
Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 25-30 minutes, or until the pudding is set and the edges are golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving warm, garnished with a sprinkle of ground mustard seeds if desired.
